description: AAA Platform — end-to-end observability for aaa-lookup-service, subscriber-profile-api, aaa-radius-server, and PostgreSQL/PgBouncer
title: Platform Overview

panels.title: Lookup Hit Rate
panels.description: Fraction of lookups resolved from cache (Stage 1). High = healthy.

panels.title: Lookup p99 Latency
panels.description: SLA target: < 15 ms. Alert threshold shown in red.

panels.title: First-Connection Rate
panels.description: Stage 2 triggers per second. Should be very low at steady state. NOTE: This metric is now emitted by the lookup pod (not the radius pod).

panels.title: Pool Exhaustion Events
panels.description: Any value > 0 in the last hour is a critical alert. Pool needs expansion.

options.content:
### Two-stage RADIUS authentication flow
`aaa-radius-server` receives UDP Access-Requests on port 1812 and resolves them in two stages:

```
Access-Request (IMSI + APN)
  │
  ├─▶ Stage 1: GET aaa-lookup-service/lookup?imsi=…&apn=…
  │     ├─ 200 → Access-Accept (static IP)              ✅  stage=1
  │     ├─ 403 → Access-Reject (suspended)              🚫  stage=1
  │     └─ 404 → Stage 2 ▼
  │
  └─▶ Stage 2: POST subscriber-profile-api/v1/first-connection
        ├─ 200/201 → Access-Accept (newly allocated IP)  ✅  stage=2
        ├─ 404/503 → Access-Reject (no pool/config)      🚫  stage=2
        └─ error   → Access-Reject                       🔴  upstream_error
```

**SLAs:** p99 end-to-end < 500 ms · Accept rate ≥ 95% · Stage 2 rate < 25% (steady state ≈ 0%)

panels.title: RADIUS Accept Rate
panels.description: Fraction of RADIUS requests resolved with Access-Accept (stage 1 + stage 2). Target â‰¥ 95%.

panels.title: RADIUS p99 Latency
panels.description: End-to-end RADIUS request latency at 99th percentile. Alert threshold: 500 ms.

panels.title: RADIUS Stage 2 Rate
panels.description: Fraction of requests that required first-connection allocation (Stage 2). High values indicate many new SIMs or range misconfigurations. NOTE: numerator now sourced from lookup pod first_connection_total counter (stage info is internal to lookup service).

panels.title: RADIUS Upstream Errors (1h)
panels.description: Unexpected HTTP errors or curl failures from aaa-lookup-service or subscriber-profile-api. Any value > 0 requires investigation. NOTE: first_connection_responses_total is now emitted by the lookup pod (not the radius pod).

panels.title: RADIUS Request Rate by Result & Stage
panels.description: Access-Accept / Access-Reject broken down by stage (1 = lookup hit, 2 = first-connection allocation). NOTE: After refactor, stage=2 is no longer emitted by the radius server; staging is now internal to the lookup service.

panels.title: RADIUS Upstream Errors
panels.description: Non-200 response rates from upstream services called by the RADIUS server. lookup = aaa-lookup-service; first-conn = subscriber-profile-api /v1/first-connection. NOTE: first_connection_responses_total is now emitted by the lookup pod (not the radius pod).

panels.title: RADIUS Request Latency Percentiles
panels.description: End-to-end RADIUS processing time including HTTP round-trips to both upstreams. SLA: p99 < 500 ms.

options.content:
### How the lookup service works
`aaa-lookup-service` is the **hot-path** of every RADIUS authentication.  Each Access-Request triggers a single `GET /lookup?imsi=…&apn=…` call (Stage 1).  The service queries the **read-replica** for a pre-allocated static IP and responds in < 15 ms (p99 SLA).

| Result | Meaning | Next step |
|--------|---------|-----------|
| `resolved` ✅ | Static IP found — subscriber known | RADIUS sends Access-Accept |
| `not_found` 🔶 | IMSI/APN not in DB yet | Falls through to Stage 2 (first-connection) |
| `suspended` 🚫 | Subscriber flagged as inactive | RADIUS sends Access-Reject immediately |
| `db_error` 🔴 | Read replica unreachable/timeout | RADIUS sends Access-Reject; triggers critical alert |

**SLAs:** p99 latency < 15 ms · DB error rate = 0 · Suspended rate < 0.05/s

panels.title: Lookup Throughput
panels.description: Total lookup requests per second across all outcomes (found, not_found, suspended, db_error).

panels.title: Lookup DB Errors (1h)
panels.description: Total DB read-replica errors in the last hour. Any value > 0 needs immediate investigation.

panels.title: Suspended Lookups (1h)
panels.description: Lookups returning 403 (subscriber suspended) in the last hour. A sustained count may indicate corrupted subscriber state or a mass-suspension batch.

panels.title: In-Flight Requests
panels.description: Current number of concurrent RADIUS requests being processed by aaa-lookup-service. Should stay well below RADIUS_WORKERS thread count.

panels.title: Lookup Request Rate by Outcome
panels.description: resolved = hot-path hit Â· not_found = triggers Stage 2 Â· suspended = SIM/IMSI inactive Â· apn_not_found = APN not in catalog

panels.title: Lookup Latency Percentiles
panels.description: SLA: p99 < 15 ms. Red threshold at 15 ms.

panels.title: In-Flight Requests
panels.description: Active GET /lookup requests being processed. Spike = backpressure.

panels.title: DB Read Replica Error Rate
panels.description: Connection failures or timeouts against the local read replica. Any sustained rate is critical.

panels.title: Not-Found Ratio (Stage 2 Trigger Rate)
panels.description: Percentage of lookups triggering Stage 2 (first-connection). Sustained spike â†’ IMSI range misconfiguration.

panels.title: Suspended vs Not-Found Lookup Rate
panels.description: Rate of suspended-subscriber rejections (403), not-found lookups (404), and DB errors per second. A rising suspended rate indicates mass-suspension or corrupted state; a rising not_found rate indicates new SIMs without matching range config.

panels.title: PostgreSQL  (CloudNativePG â€” built-in metrics exporter on port 9187)

options.content:
### PostgreSQL architecture (CloudNativePG)
The database cluster runs as a **CloudNativePG** managed resource. Metrics are scraped via a **PodMonitor** from the built-in CNPG metrics exporter on port **9187** (`cnpg_*` prefix).

```
subscriber-profile-api ──▶ PgBouncer (RW pooler) ──▶ Primary   (reads + writes)
aaa-lookup-service     ──────────────────────────▶ Replica    (read-only, low latency)
```

| Metric | Source | Key threshold |
|--------|--------|---------------|
| `cnpg_collector_up` | CNPG exporter | Must be 1 — if 0, primary is unreachable |
| `cnpg_pg_replication_lag` | Standby node | < 30 s — stale replica = stale lookup results |
| `cnpg_backends_total{state}` | Primary | `idle in transaction` > 5 = lock hazard |
| `cnpg_pg_database_size_bytes{datname="aaa"}` | Primary | Growth > 20%/24 h = investigate retention |
| `cnpg_pg_stat_database_blks_*` | Primary | Cache hit ratio < 95% = I/O bottleneck |
| `cnpg_pg_stat_database_deadlocks` | Primary | Deadlock rate > 0 = concurrent write contention |

**Recovery:** CloudNativePG auto-promotes the replica to primary within ~30 s if the primary fails.
**Enable monitoring:** set `aaa-database.postgresql.monitoring.enabled: true` in your values file.

panels.title: DB Primary Up
panels.description: `cnpg_collector_up` = 1 when the CNPG metrics exporter is connected to the primary. 0 = primary unreachable.

panels.title: Active DB Connections
panels.description: Connections currently executing a query on the primary. High values indicate slow queries or connection pile-up.

panels.title: Replica Lag (s)
panels.description: Replication lag in **seconds** as reported by CloudNativePG's built-in exporter (`cnpg_pg_replication_lag`). Yellow ≥ 10 s, Red ≥ 30 s. Only populated on standby nodes; shows 0 or no data on the primary.

panels.title: Database Size
panels.description: Total on-disk size of the aaa database. Yellow >= 5 GB, Red >= 10 GB.

panels.title: DB Connection States (by pg_stat_activity)
panels.description: Backend connection counts per state from `cnpg_backends_total` (CloudNativePG built-in exporter). `idle in transaction` sessions hold locks; a growing count indicates long-running transactions that need investigation.

panels.title: Transaction Rate (commits + rollbacks)
panels.description: Committed and rolled-back transactions per second on the aaa database. A spike in rollbacks indicates application-level conflicts or failed bulk-job rows.

panels.title: Replication Lag Over Time (seconds)
panels.description: Replication lag trend in seconds over time. SLA threshold: 30 s. Sustained lag means the replica is falling behind under write load.

panels.title: DB Block Cache Hit Ratio
panels.description: Percentage of block reads served from shared_buffers (no disk I/O). Values below 95% indicate the server needs more shared_buffers or RAM.

panels.title: DB Deadlocks & Conflicts
panels.description: Rate of deadlocks and replication conflicts on the `aaa` database. Deadlock spikes indicate concurrent write transactions that are blocking each other. Conflicts occur when a standby replica cancels a query due to WAL conflicts.

options.content:
### What the Subscriber Profile API manages
Python/FastAPI service (port 8080) responsible for all **provisioning** operations.

| Operation | Endpoint | Notes |
|-----------|----------|-------|
| First-connection | `POST /v1/first-connection` | Called by RADIUS Stage 2; allocates static IP from pool |
| Bulk import | `POST /v1/bulk-jobs` + CSV | One IMSI per row; rows with same ICCID merged into one profile |
| IP pools | `GET/POST /v1/pools` | Manage IPv4 subnets; pre-populate IPs on creation |
| Range configs | `GET/POST /v1/range-configs` | Map IMSI prefix ranges → pool; optional per-APN overrides |
| Profiles | `GET/DELETE /v1/profiles/{imsi}` | Per-subscriber IP + suspension status |

**SLAs:** First-connection p99 < 200 ms · Bulk failure rate < 5% · Pool exhaustion = 0

panels.title: API Request Latency Percentiles
panels.description: All endpoints combined. Separate by method/path with the panel below for drill-down.

panels.title: API Throughput by Method + Path
panels.description: Request rate per endpoint. Useful for identifying hot paths and regressions.

panels.title: First-Connection Outcomes
panels.description: allocated = new SIM provisioned Â· reused = idempotent re-request Â· not_found = IMSI outside all range configs Â· pool_exhausted = critical NOTE: This metric is now emitted by the lookup pod (not the radius pod).

panels.title: Pool Exhaustion Events by Pool
panels.description: Any rate > 0 for any pool_id is a critical alert â€” pool needs immediate expansion.

panels.title: Bulk Job Profile Throughput
panels.description: Rows processed vs failed per second across all active bulk jobs.

panels.title: Bulk Job Duration Percentiles
panels.description: End-to-end processing time for bulk import jobs. Buckets: 1s â†’ 600s.

panels.title: Multi-IMSI Siblings Provisioned Rate
panels.description: Rate at which sibling IMSIs are pre-provisioned in the same first-connection transaction. Tracks multi-IMSI SIM thundering-herd protection.

panels.title: Management UI  (aaa-management-ui Â· RUM metrics via Pushgateway)

options.content:
### Real User Monitoring (RUM) — how it works
The management UI (`aaa-management-ui`) instruments every axios API call with timing and error tracking.  Metrics are accumulated **in-memory** and pushed to the **Prometheus Pushgateway** every 30 seconds  (and on page unload) in Prometheus text format.

| Metric | Type | What it tracks |
|--------|------|----------------|
| `ui_api_call_requests_total{endpoint}` | counter | Total API calls per endpoint template |
| `ui_api_call_duration_ms_total{endpoint}` | counter | Cumulative call duration (ms) per endpoint |
| `ui_api_call_errors_total{endpoint}` | counter | Non-2xx or network failures per endpoint |

**Endpoint templates** collapse dynamic IDs: `/profiles/abc-123` → `/profiles/:id`.
**Note:** Metrics are only collected while the UI tab is open. 429 errors are auto-retried (3×, exponential back-off) and not counted as errors.

panels.title: UI API Call Rate
panels.description: Total frontend-observed API calls per second across all endpoints.

panels.title: UI API Error Rate (1h)
panels.description: Frontend-observed API call errors (non-2xx or network failure) in the last hour. Any persistent rate > 0 warrants investigation.

panels.title: UI Avg API Latency
panels.description: Average frontend-observed API call duration across all endpoints.

panels.title: UI API Throughput by Endpoint
panels.description: Frontend-observed API call rate per endpoint template. Helps identify which pages are most active.

panels.title: UI API Avg Latency by Endpoint
panels.description: Average API call duration as observed in the browser, broken down by endpoint. High values indicate slow API responses or network issues.

panels.title: UI API Error Rate by Endpoint
panels.description: Frontend-observed API errors (non-2xx or network) per endpoint. Sustained errors may indicate backend issues or UI bugs.
